output.userFeedback: When the accordion can't be closed (always-one-open preventing the last item from closing), clicking it still shows a close animation and then it opens again.

issueAnalysis.description: The current implementation listens to the 'toggle' event which fires AFTER the native toggle animation has already occurred. When alwaysOneOpen is enabled and the last open item is clicked, the native close animation plays, then JavaScript re-opens it programmatically, creating a jarring visual experience.
issueAnalysis.rootCause: The toggle event is fired after the <details> element has already toggled its state. By the time we detect that the item should stay open and re-open it, the close animation has already played.
issueAnalysis.solution: Prevent the default toggle behavior by listening to the 'click' event on the <summary> element and calling event.preventDefault() BEFORE the toggle occurs, when alwaysOneOpen is enabled and the clicked item is the only open item.

changesImplemented.description: Added click event listener on summary elements to prevent closing the last open item
changesImplemented.implementation: Before the toggle event listener, added a click listener that checks if: (1) alwaysOneOpen is enabled, (2) the clicked detail is currently open, (3) no other siblings are open. If all conditions are true, calls event.preventDefault() to prevent the native toggle from occurring.
changesImplemented.reasoning: The click event fires before the toggle event, allowing us to prevent the default behavior before any animation occurs. This eliminates the close-then-reopen flash.

changesImplemented.description: Simplified toggle event listener by removing redundant re-open logic
changesImplemented.implementation: Removed the 'else if (alwaysOneOpen)' block that was re-opening closed items. This logic is now unnecessary because the click handler prevents closing in the first place.
changesImplemented.reasoning: With the click handler preventing invalid closes, the toggle event only needs to handle closing siblings when exclusive mode is enabled. This simplifies the code and improves performance.

filesModified.purpose: Added click event prevention logic to eliminate close animation flash when alwaysOneOpen prevents closing the last item

alwaysOneOpen.scenario: Click the only open item when alwaysOneOpen=true
alwaysOneOpen.expected: Item stays open with no animation
alwaysOneOpen.implementation: event.preventDefault() prevents the native toggle, so no close animation occurs

exclusiveMode.scenario: Open a different item when one is already open
exclusiveMode.expected: Previously open item closes, newly clicked item opens
exclusiveMode.implementation: Toggle event listener closes siblings when an item opens. Click handler does not prevent this scenario.

regularToggle.scenario: Open/close items when alwaysOneOpen=false or multiple items are open
regularToggle.expected: Items open and close normally with native animation
regularToggle.implementation: Click handler only prevents when alwaysOneOpen=true AND no siblings are open. All other cases proceed normally.

autoOpenFirst.scenario: Page load with alwaysOneOpen=true and no items open
autoOpenFirst.expected: First item auto-opens on page load
autoOpenFirst.implementation: Unchanged - existing logic in lines 55-60 still handles this case

relevantVariations.testInstructions: Click 'Item A' when it's the only open item. Verify no close animation occurs and item stays open.
Verify first item auto-opens on page load. Click it and verify no close animation. Open 'Second Item' then try to close it - should stay open with no animation.
Verify exclusive mode still works - opening one item closes the other, with normal animations.
Verify normal accordion behavior unchanged - items open/close with normal animations.

output.changesSummary: Fixed the close animation flash when alwaysOneOpen prevents closing the last item. Added click event listener on summary elements that calls event.preventDefault() BEFORE the toggle occurs when the item is the only open item and alwaysOneOpen is enabled. Removed redundant re-open logic from toggle listener since prevention eliminates the need to re-open. Build passes successfully.
